description: Turn the deck builder into a sales intelligence platform. Public share URLs (/d/[slug]), tokenized lead tracking, mobile-responsive viewing, per-slide analytics, engagement notifications. Built on the slide-as-website architecture with server presentation routes and media proxy.

userStories.title: Database migration — share settings, tokens, views
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables for share settings, viewer tokens, and view analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_create_deck_sharing_tables.sql
Add share_settings JSONB column to slide_templates with default null

Schema: { enabled: boolean, slug: string, expiry: string|null, gate_type: 'none'|'email'|'password', gate_value: string|null, cta_enabled: boolean, cta_text: string|null, cta_url: string|null, show_branding: boolean }
Create deck_share_tokens table: id UUID PK, template_id FK, lead_id FK nullable, email TEXT NOT NULL, token TEXT UNIQUE, parent_lead_id UUID nullable, created_at, expires_at nullable

Create deck_views table: id UUID PK, template_id FK, token_id FK nullable, viewer_email nullable, device_type TEXT, slides_viewed JSONB, total_duration_seconds INT, completion_rate DECIMAL, viewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ip_address TEXT nullable
Add indexes on deck_share_tokens(token), deck_share_tokens(template_id), deck_views(template_id, viewed_at), deck_views(token_id)
Add html_mobile optional field to SlideData type in src/types/deck-builder.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read supabase/migrations/ for existing migration patterns. Read src/types/deck-builder.ts for SlideData type (add html_mobile alongside media_refs). Use supabase-expert skill for migration guidance. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Share settings API routes (GET + PATCH)
userStories.description: As an admin, I need API endpoints to get and update share settings for a template.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/share/route.ts
GET returns current share_settings from template or default { enabled: false }
PATCH updates share_settings JSONB on the template row
Auto-generate slug from template name on first enable (kebab-case, unique). If conflict, append -XXXX random suffix
Validate: slug is URL-safe (lowercase, hyphens only), expiry is valid ISO date or null, gate_type is 'none'|'email'|'password'
Admin JWT auth required on both routes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/app/api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/route.ts for existing auth pattern (verifyAdminToken). Use crypto.randomBytes for suffix. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Public viewer page — /d/[slug] (basic rendering)
userStories.description: As a viewer, I want to open a shared deck URL and see the presentation without logging in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/d/[slug]/page.tsx as a server component
Add /d to middleware exclusions in proxy.ts or middleware.ts so auth doesn't block
Fetch template by slug: slide_templates where share_settings->>'slug' = slug AND share_settings->>'enabled' = 'true'
Check expiry — if expired, render 'This link has expired' page
If gate_type is 'none', render deck directly
Render by fetching presentation HTML server-side from /api/slide/[templateId]/present using internal fetch
Include og:title (deck name), og:description
No admin navigation, no app chrome — full-screen presentation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the BASIC version — no gates, no token handling, no tracking. Those come in later stories. Use the existing /api/slide/[templateId]/present route. Read src/middleware.ts or proxy.ts for middleware exclusion patterns. Use nextjs-expert agent for the server component.

userStories.title: Gate pages — email capture and password protection
userStories.description: As a viewer, when a shared deck requires email or password, I should see the appropriate gate before the deck.
acceptanceCriteria: In /d/[slug]/page.tsx, if gate_type is 'password', render a password form page (terminal-style, dark theme)
Password form submits to a client action that validates against share_settings.gate_value
On correct password, render the deck (could use URL param or session cookie)
If gate_type is 'email', render email capture form
On email submit, auto-create a lead with status 'pending' in leads table
Create a deck_share_tokens row for that email
Then render the deck with the token embedded for tracking
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read existing lead creation patterns in src/app/api/admin/leads/. The gate forms should match the terminal/dark aesthetic. Use frontend-expert agent for the gate UI components.

userStories.title: OG image generation endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that returns a screenshot of slide 1 for social preview cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/deck/[slug]/og-image/route.ts — no auth required
Fetch template by slug, navigate Playwright to /api/slide/[templateId]/0, capture screenshot
Return PNG with Content-Type: image/png and Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600
If template not found or sharing disabled, return default 1200x630 Claru-branded placeholder
Viewport: 1200x630 for standard og:image size
Reuse captureSlideScreenshotFromUrl from src/lib/deck-builder/slide-capture.ts
Add og:image meta tag to /d/[slug] page pointing to this endpoint
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/deck-builder/slide-capture.ts for captureSlideScreenshotFromUrl. Set viewport to 1200x630. For the placeholder, create a simple HTML page with Claru branding and screenshot that. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Analytics tracking script and API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need lightweight JS tracking and an API to record viewer engagement.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/deck/[slug]/track/route.ts — no auth, POST only
Accepts: { token?: string, event: 'view'|'slide_change'|'complete', slide_index?: number, duration?: number, device?: string, view_id?: string }
On 'view': create deck_views row, return { view_id }
On 'slide_change': update slides_viewed JSONB array (append slide entry)
On 'complete': update total_duration_seconds and completion_rate
Create src/lib/deck-builder/tracking-script.ts exporting a string of vanilla JS (<50 lines)
Script sends beacon on load (view), slide transition (slide_change), page unload (complete)
Script detects device type from UA, reads ?t= token from URL
No cookies, no localStorage — stateless beacon API
Inject the tracking script into /d/[slug] page HTML
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tracking script is injected by the /d/[slug] server component after fetching the presentation HTML. Use navigator.sendBeacon with fetch keepalive fallback. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Token validation and attribution in viewer page
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the viewer page to validate ?t= tokens and attribute views to leads.
acceptanceCriteria: In /d/[slug]/page.tsx, check for ?t=[token] query param
If present, validate against deck_share_tokens table (check token exists, not expired, matches template)
If valid, pass token to the tracking script context
If invalid or expired, still render deck but don't attribute (anonymous view)
Tracking script includes token in all beacon payloads
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This wires US-003 (viewer page) + US-006 (tracking) together with token validation. Read deck_share_tokens table schema from US-001. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Share panel — basic settings UI
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a Share drawer in the editor to configure sharing settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/deck-builder/SharePanel.tsx — slide-out drawer from right
Triggered by a Share button in the editor header bar
Toggle: sharing enabled/disabled
Slug field: editable text input, shows full URL {SITE_URL}/d/{slug}
Copy link button — copies URL to clipboard with toast
Expiry dropdown: Never, 7 days, 30 days, Custom date
Gate selector: None, Email Capture, Password (with password input)
CTA toggle: enabled/disabled with text + URL inputs
Branding toggle: show/hide Powered by Claru
Uses GET/PATCH share settings API from US-002
Matches terminal/monospace aesthetic
Dismiss with X or Escape
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read src/app/components/deck-builder/VersionHistory.tsx for drawer pattern. Read DeckEditorClient.tsx for header button placement. Match the terminal aesthetic — font-mono, dark bg, sage green accent. Use frontend-expert agent. Use Shadcn UI Designer agent for the form components.

userStories.title: Analytics summary API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need an endpoint that returns aggregate analytics for a deck.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GET /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/analytics — admin JWT auth
Returns: { total_views, unique_viewers, avg_completion_rate, views_by_day: [{date, count}], top_slides: [{index, avg_duration}], device_breakdown: {desktop, mobile, tablet} }
total_views: COUNT of deck_views
unique_viewers: COUNT DISTINCT of (token_id, viewer_email, ip_address)
avg_completion_rate: AVG of completion_rate
views_by_day: GROUP BY date, last 30 days
top_slides: sorted by avg duration
device_breakdown: GROUP BY device_type
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Heavy Supabase SQL queries. Use supabase-expert skill for query optimization. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Analytics display in Share panel
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see basic analytics inline on the share panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add analytics section to SharePanel.tsx (below settings, above lead sharing)
Fetch from GET /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/analytics
Show: Total views, Unique viewers, Avg completion rate as stat cards
Show views_by_day as a simple sparkline or bar chart (last 30 days)
Show device breakdown as percentages
Loading state while fetching
Empty state: 'No views yet' when total_views is 0
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use frontend-expert agent. Simple stat cards + mini chart. No charting library needed — can use SVG sparkline or CSS bars.

userStories.title: Lead list share — send API and token creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API to create tokens and send share emails to leads.
acceptanceCriteria: Create POST /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/share/send
Accepts: { lead_ids?: string[], emails?: string[], parent_lead_id?: string }
For each lead_id: look up lead email, create deck_share_tokens row with crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex'), send email via Resend
For each email: create token row with lead_id null, parent_lead_id if provided, send email
Email template: branded dark theme, includes lead name, deck title, tokenized link
Subject: 'A presentation from Claru AI'
Returns: { sent: number, tokens: [{email, token, url}] }
Admin JWT auth required
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/email.ts or src/lib/email/ for existing Resend integration and branded email templates. Read src/lib/deck-builder/agents/executor.ts line ~274 for lead lookup patterns. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Lead share UI in Share panel
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to select leads and send them tracked share links from the Share panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Share to Leads' section in SharePanel below analytics
Search/select leads autocomplete (fetch from leads table by name or email)
For each selected lead, show name + email + 'Send' button
Bulk 'Send All' button for multiple leads
Calls POST /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/share/send with lead_ids
Shows success toast with count sent
Previously shared leads shown with 'Resend' option
Add 'Invite by Email' section: free-form email input (comma-separated)
Optional parent lead dropdown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use frontend-expert agent. Autocomplete can be a simple input with debounced fetch. Show shared leads list from GET /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/share (which should also return existing tokens).

userStories.title: Per-viewer analytics on lead detail page
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see deck engagement on a lead's detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create GET /api/admin/leads/[id]/deck-views — returns views attributed to this lead
On LeadDetailClient page, add 'Deck Engagement' section
Shows: list of decks viewed, viewed date, completion rate, time spent, slides count
Click to expand: slide-by-slide breakdown with duration per slide
Sort by most recent first
Empty state: 'No deck engagement yet'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read src/app/admin/leads/[id]/LeadDetailClient.tsx for the existing lead detail page structure. Use frontend-expert agent for the engagement section UI.

userStories.title: Mobile-responsive CSS layer for shared decks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared decks to be usable on mobile phones.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @media (max-width: 768px) block to /api/slide/[templateId]/present route CSS
Mobile: fluid layout instead of fixed 1920x1080, scale text proportionally, increase touch padding
Restack two-column layouts to vertical on mobile
Progress: dots instead of bar on mobile
Hide hover-only elements
Bigger touch targets for navigation
In presentation route: if slide.html_mobile exists AND mobile UA, use it instead of slide.html
Fallback: responsive CSS layer on desktop HTML when no html_mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using Playwright at 375x812 viewport
userStories.notes: Read src/app/api/slide/[templateId]/present/route.ts for the presentation CSS. The responsive CSS goes in the same <style> block. UA detection via request headers. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Engagement notification emails
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to be notified when a lead views my shared deck.
acceptanceCriteria: In POST /api/deck/[slug]/track, when 'view' event has a valid token linked to a lead:
Send notification email via Resend to ADMIN_EMAIL
Email: '{Lead Name} ({Company}) viewed your deck {Deck Name}'
Include link to the deck editor share panel
Debounce: max 1 notification per lead per deck per hour (check deck_views for recent views)
Email template matches Claru branding (dark theme, sage green)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/email/ for Resend integration. MEMORY.md says email template bg #0a0908, card #121210, border #2a2a28, accent #92B090. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — public share flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test for deck sharing and viewing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-sharing.spec.ts
Admin enables sharing via PATCH API (slug: test-share)
Navigate to /d/test-share (no auth) — verify presentation renders
Verify og:image meta tag present
Navigate slides with keyboard
Verify deck_views has a new row via API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use webapp-testing skill with Playwright MCP. Use login pattern from tests/e2e/deck-builder-crud.spec.ts.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — lead share with analytics
userStories.description: As QA, I need a test for tokenized lead sharing and per-viewer analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-lead-sharing.spec.ts
Create template + lead via API, enable sharing, create token
Open /d/[slug]?t=[token] — verify renders
Navigate 3 slides, close page
Fetch /api/admin/leads/[id]/deck-views — verify view attributed
Verify slides_viewed has 3 entries, completion_rate calculated
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use webapp-testing skill with Playwright MCP.

userStories.title: Handle inline edit PostMessage and update slide data
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to double-click text in the slide preview to edit it inline so I can make quick text changes without using the chat.
acceptanceCriteria: Add inlineEdit handler to the PostMessage listener in DeckEditorClient.tsx alongside the existing elementSelected handler
PostMessage payload: { type: 'inlineEdit', tag: string, innerHTML: string, originalInnerHTML: string, selector: string }
Compare innerHTML with originalInnerHTML — skip if identical

For custom HTML slides (slide.html exists): find the element in the HTML string by matching originalInnerHTML content, use regex to find opening tag matching the tag name and replace originalInnerHTML with new innerHTML
If multiple regex matches, use the selector hint (nth-of-type) to disambiguate
For layout slides (no slide.html): if tag is h1/h2/h3 → strip HTML tags, update slide.title; if tag is p/li/span → strip HTML tags, update slide.body
Call updateCurrentSlide() with the changed data
Show toast 'Text updated' via existing showToast
If element not found in HTML string, show toast 'Could not apply edit — use chat instead'
Add INLINE_EDIT_SCRIPT constant injected into iframe srcdoc for both custom HTML and layout slides
Script handles dblclick on text elements (h1-h3, p, li, span, td, th, a, label, blockquote), makes contenteditable, sends inlineEdit PostMessage on blur/Enter
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The INLINE_EDIT_SCRIPT is injected alongside SELECTOR_SCRIPT for custom HTML slides, and appended before </body> for layout slides. Uses refs (slidesRef, selectedIndexRef) to avoid stale closures in the message handler.

userStories.title: Enable pointer events on center preview iframe
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to be able to double-click text in the slide preview to trigger inline editing.
acceptanceCriteria: In CenterSlidePreview, the iframe had: className with conditional pointer-events-none based on selectorMode
Change to: always pointer-events auto (remove the conditional pointer-events-none entirely)
This allows double-click to reach the iframe content for inline editing
Single clicks inside the iframe don't bubble to the parent (iframe boundary), so this doesn't break slide selection
The iframe remains scrollable at zoom levels > Fit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple CSS change: remove the pointer-events-none conditional from the iframe className in CenterSlidePreview.
